title: Spain ratifies Gibraltar deal but insists no change in sovereignty claims over the territory
content: The new deal officially brings to an end the final chapter of the UK's exit process from the EU in a deal set to boost cooperation and freedoms in the disputed territory.
snippet: The new deal officially brings to an end the final chapter of the UK's exit process from the EU in a deal set to boost cooperation and freedoms in the disputed territory.

title: National sovereignty equals 'sending gifts to US and China', former Italian PM Letta tells Euronews
content: The former Italian prime minister tells Euronews that, to be able to compete with Washington and Beijing, the EU must keep a united front, adding that "the true battle is for European sovereignty".
snippet: The former Italian prime minister tells Euronews that, to be able to compete with Washington and Beijing, the EU must keep a united front, adding that "the true battle is for European sovereignty".

title: Euronews explains: can Hungary's PM Magyar really remove president Sulyok from office?
content: Hungary's parliament has approved a constitutional amendment to remove President Tamás Sulyok from office, with Prime Minister Péter Magyar accusing him of being Orbán's puppet. But he refuses to resign and argues the move in unconstitutional. What happens next? Euronews explains:

title: Hungary risks missing early Erasmus+ return despite EU promises
content: Budapest may miss the September deadline to rejoin the programme unless it submits rule-of-law reforms to Brussels on schedule.
snippet: Budapest may miss the September deadline to rejoin the programme unless it submits rule-of-law reforms to Brussels on schedule.

title: Several reported dead after fire in lift shaft of Brussels construction site
content: Local media reports say firefighters initially brought the blaze under control, but flames spread into a lift shaft, sparking a second fire and trapping people inside.
snippet: Local media reports say firefighters initially brought the blaze under control, but flames spread into a lift shaft, sparking a second fire and trapping people inside.

title: Ukraine forces hit more than 100 Russian vessels in Sea of Azov in eight days
content: Russia is looking for a way to redirect ships from the Sea of Azov after Ukrainian forces struck more than 100 Russian vessels — including oil tankers and cargo ships — in less than two weeks, isolating Moscow-occupied Crimea even more.

title: Government reshuffle in Ukraine: Parliament dismisses Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko
content: After Ukraine's parliament accepted the prime minister's resignation, the composition of the next wartime cabinet remains unclear. It is not yet known which ministers will retain their posts across key portfolios.

title: Non-EU citizens most likely to have temporary and part-time work in the bloc
content: Non-EU citizens in the bloc face higher rates of temporary and part-time jobs compared to EU nationals, with some countries showing greater disparities than others. What factors are behind it?
snippet: Non-EU citizens in the bloc face higher rates of temporary and part-time jobs compared to EU nationals, with some countries showing greater disparities than others. What factors are behind it?

title: EU dismantles tax evasion network in Greece with raids in Athens and Kastoria
content: The criminal network is estimated to have inflicted losses amounting to at least 46.9 million euros.
snippet: The criminal network is estimated to have inflicted losses amounting to at least 46.9 million euros.

title: Spanish PM's brother banned from public office for nine years for misconduct
content: David Sánchez, a composer and orchestra director whose career has included training and stops in Saint Petersburg, Toulouse, Tokyo and Madrid, was tried with 10 other defendants.
snippet: David Sánchez, a composer and orchestra director whose career has included training and stops in Saint Petersburg, Toulouse, Tokyo and Madrid, was tried with 10 other defendants.

title: Brussels warns dialogue with China 'will not suffice'
content: EU trade enforcer Denis Redonnet told MEPs on Tuesday that the European Commission will act against cheap Chinese imports by adopting "unilateral" defence measures before the October deadline set by EU trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič to make progress in the negotiation with China.

title: Almost 60 people gored or bruised during Spain's famed San Fermín bull runs
content: Sixteen people have been killed in the bull runs since records started in 1911. The last death was in 2009 when a bull gored a 27-year-old Spaniard in the neck, heart and lungs.
snippet: Sixteen people have been killed in the bull runs since records started in 1911. The last death was in 2009 when a bull gored a 27-year-old Spaniard in the neck, heart and lungs.

title: Fact check: FIFA boss Infantino probe called for by MEPs, not whole parliament
content: Viral posts falsely claim that the European Parliament called for an investigation into FIFA's President Gianni Infantino.
snippet: Viral posts falsely claim that the European Parliament called for an investigation into FIFA's President Gianni Infantino.

title: Fontainebleau fire still burning as 850 firefighters tackle blaze
content: The fire in the Fontainebleau forest, around sixty kilometres from Paris, is still spreading. Around 850 firefighters, 200 vehicles and six aircraft have been deployed.
snippet: The fire in the Fontainebleau forest, around sixty kilometres from Paris, is still spreading. Around 850 firefighters, 200 vehicles and six aircraft have been deployed.

title: Ukraine inches closer towards joining EU with opening of another cluster
content: Ministers in Brussels approved the opening of another portion of Ukrainian accession negotiations on Tuesday. The decision continues the erosion of a two-year deadlock caused by the former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán.

content: Velislava Petrova-Chamova told Euronews that the EU’s sanctions against Russia should have a financial impact and not be a symbolic instrument – and that this, among other reasons, is why Sofia championed the removal of the head of Russia's Orthodox Church from the 21st sanctions package.

title: France flaunts firepower and unity with allies as Macron hosts final Bastille Day parade
content: With nearly 6,700 troops, 98 aircraft, 31 helicopters and 315 vehicles, a record number of military personnel were marching the traditional route between the Arc de Triomphe and the Place de la Concorde.

title: EU pushes to triple energy storage as renewable power goes to waste
content: EU energy ministers signed the first-ever tripartite agreement to boost the bloc’s energy storage capacity on 26 June. The EU needs 200 gigawatt (GW) of storage capacity by 2030, compared with the current 55. 22 member states promised to add around 30-35 GW of new capacity by 2028.

title: Russian army executed hundreds of Ukrainian POWs since 2022, Kyiv says
content: A UN report from last month cited 129 verified executions of Ukrainian prisoners of war, with the organisation sounding the alarm last year over a "marked increase" in cases.
snippet: A UN report from last month cited 129 verified executions of Ukrainian prisoners of war, with the organisation sounding the alarm last year over a "marked increase" in cases.

title: Europe Today: Tensions high in the Middle East as Trump vows 20% Hormuz shipping fee
content: The tensions in the Persian Gulf remain high. US President Trump said the United States would take over the Strait of Hormuz and charge a fee of 20% for all cargo shipped through the waterway. Earlier, Trump declared the strait will remain open, “with or without Iran”.
snippet: The tensions in the Persian Gulf remain high. US President Trump said the United States would take over the Strait of Hormuz and charge a fee of 20% for all cargo shipped through the waterway.

title: Future of the VW Osnabrück plant: Is the Iron Dome missile deal at risk of collapse?
content: Vehicle production at VW in Osnabrück is scheduled to end in 2027, meaning around 2,300 jobs depend on a follow-up solution.
snippet: Vehicle production at VW in Osnabrück is scheduled to end in 2027, meaning around 2,300 jobs depend on a follow-up solution.

title: Summer skiers head to melting glacier as heatwave grips France
content: Hundreds of skiers headed to the Grande Motte glacier in Tignes to escape France's heatwave and enjoy summer skiing. But visitors and experts say the glacier is shrinking rapidly, with scientists warning it could disappear within 10 to 15 years.

title: Hungarian parliament removes President Tamás Sulyok from office
content: Hungary's parliament has approved the 17th amendment to the constitution. Prime Mİnister Péter Magyar says this is a key step that will allow 'Orbán's political puppets' to be replaced.
snippet: Hungary's parliament has approved the 17th amendment to the constitution. Prime Mİnister Péter Magyar says this is a key step that will allow 'Orbán's political puppets' to be replaced.

title: Full ban on Israeli settlement trade gets ‘most support’ from EU countries, Kallas says
content: EU foreign ministers overwhelmingly backed clamping down on EU trade with Israeli settlements during closed-door discussions on Monday, with an official source saying the biggest cohort of countries supported passing new measures without requiring the unanimous backing of all member states.

title: UK joins EU's €90 billion loan for Ukraine with promise of 'fair' contribution
content: London has agreed to join in the long-in-the-making EU plan to give Ukraine a much-needed financial boost.
snippet: London has agreed to join in the long-in-the-making EU plan to give Ukraine a much-needed financial boost.

title: Nine European countries and Ukraine form anti-ballistic missile coalition
content: The creation of the anti-ballistic missile coalition comes in the wake of a growing threat of ballistic missiles on Europe. The declaration states the coalition serves in a 'purely defensive' manner.
snippet: The creation of the anti-ballistic missile coalition comes in the wake of a growing threat of ballistic missiles on Europe. The declaration states the coalition serves in a 'purely defensive' manner.

title: EU sanctions Russia's VK Company for helping expose Putin's critics
content: The EU has sanctioned VK Company for aiding the repression of critics of President Vladimir Putin and his war against Ukraine. The company says its services remain "available to users as normal".
snippet: The EU has sanctioned VK Company for aiding the repression of critics of President Vladimir Putin and his war against Ukraine. The company says its services remain "available to users as normal".

title: Rajoy's accusation that France's team play 'without the french' sparks racism row

title: US Congress battles over allowing Turkey to return to the F-35 programme

snippet: While the White House has signalled a desire to work more closely with Ankara, some members of Congress have raised concerns about plans to allow Turkey to rejoin the fighter jet programme.

title: Doctors Without Borders slams 'deliberate' Russian destruction of Ukraine's hospitals
content: The World Health Organisation recorded 2,815 attacks on healthcare in Ukraine between the February 2022 full-scale invasion and the end of 2025, with the vast majority involving violence with heavy weapons.

title: Shocking footage: Ex-deputy minister in Poland's former ruling party shown helping Russian soldiers
content: Krzysztof Tołwiński, a former deputy treasury minister in a PiS government, released footage from Belarus saying he had given first-aid kits to Russian troops fighting in Ukraine.
snippet: Krzysztof Tołwiński, a former deputy treasury minister in a PiS government, released footage from Belarus saying he had given first-aid kits to Russian troops fighting in Ukraine.

title: Singapore court postpones French teen's public nuisance straw-licking case to 30 July

title: No, the EU is not about to censor access to the internet
content: Viral claims that the EU is introducing an "internet passport" to control or censor internet access are misleading. They misrepresent the European Commission's planned age-verification app.
snippet: Viral claims that the EU is introducing an "internet passport" to control or censor internet access are misleading. They misrepresent the European Commission's planned age-verification app.

title: Chance find in Nordholz: Nazi military Sturmgeschütz III assault gun unearthed
content: The almost completely intact vehicle was found during construction work in Nordholz. It is due to be moved to the German Tank Museum in Munster in August.
snippet: The almost completely intact vehicle was found during construction work in Nordholz. It is due to be moved to the German Tank Museum in Munster in August.

title: EU fails again to sanction Patriarch Kirill as Bulgaria slaps veto

title: Food security is 'at the forefront' of everybody's mind, Irish agriculture minister says

title: Why is Europe still wasting clean energy? Take our poll
content: Europe is producing more renewable electricity than ever before, but much of it cannot be used when demand is highest. For that, EU governments have launched a new push to expand energy storage, hoping to make clean power reliable and reduce dependence on fossil fuels.

snippet: Funds will be used to create more police patrols outside synagogues and Jewish schools following a spate of hate attacks amid growing antisemitism.

title: Anti-tracking fashion: Startup designs clothing to foil AI surveillance cameras
